config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas yang kuat, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diutamakan tanpa kontrol, itu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan terhadap orang yang lemah atau minoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang tetap menghormati kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan hukum setiap orang. Dalam praktiknya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu-individu yang haknya aman.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my American perspective, that kind of rule is dangerous because it can become a blank check for governments or majorities to trample unpopular people, minorities, or dissenters in the name of the "greater good." Society does have legitimate claims, but those claims have to be limited by strong rights, due process, and constitutional protections. In practice, a healthy society is not built by sacrificing individuals too easily; it is built by protecting their rights while balancing real public needs carefully.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk menekan suara yang lemah, minoritas, atau orang yang tidak punya akses kuasa, jadi kalau hak individu tidak jadi batas yang tegas, penyalahgunaan sangat mudah terjadi. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tapi harus dibatasi oleh hukum, keadilan, dan perlindungan hak dasar, bukan dijadikan alasan otomatis untuk mengorbankan orang per orang. Justru masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang bisa mengejar kebaikan bersama tanpa menginjak martabat individunya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still reject the idea that society should take priority over the individual as a general rule. From a U.S. perspective, history makes me wary of any argument that says the collective good should come first, because that logic has repeatedly been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and abuse of unpopular groups. That does not mean individual rights are absolute in every case, but the burden should be on society to prove a very strong, limited necessity before overriding them. In my view, a free society is strongest when it treats individual rights as the default and collective interests as something that must be carefully constrained.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus menjadi prioritas umum di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai sebagai pembenaran untuk membungkam yang lemah, jadi kalau hak individu tidak ditempatkan sebagai batas yang keras, penyalahgunaan akan mudah terjadi. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti bencana atau kesehatan publik di mana pembatasan sementara bisa dibenarkan, tetapi itu harus sangat ketat, proporsional, dan bisa diawasi. Jadi, kepentingan bersama penting, tetapi tidak boleh dijadikan alasan otomatis untuk mengorbankan martabat dan kebebasan orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still come down against making society’s interests the default priority over individual rights. In the United States, I have seen how easily “the common good” can become a cover for censorship, discrimination, and punishing people who are unpopular or powerless, so I do not trust that principle as a general rule. Yes, there are narrow cases like public health or emergencies where limited restrictions make sense, but those need to be exceptional, proportional, and tightly checked. My view is that individual rights should be the starting point, because once you let the collective override them too easily, the people with the least power usually pay the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
